chief_complaint: A 35-year-old male presented with fever, headache, and vomiting, which gradually progressed to unconsciousness.
history: The patient was admitted to the hospital due to fever, headache, and vomiting. Cerebrospinal fluid examination revealed haemorrhagic suppurative meningitis, and Hongkiin second-generation sequencing of cerebrospinal fluid and blood pathogens resulted in the detection of Naegleria fowleri; thus, a diagnosis of primary amebic meningitis was made. The patient had no known medical history and was not taking any medications. Social history was not provided.
exam: Vital signs: fever, tachycardia, and tachypnea. Physical examination revealed a comatose patient with signs of meningismus.
labs: ECG: not specified. Bloods: Naegleria fowleri detected in cerebrospinal fluid and blood. Imaging: not specified. Bedside transthoracic echocardiography revealed signs of acute myocarditis.
correct_diagnosis: Primary amebic meningitis with acute myocarditis
differentials: Primary amebic meningitis with acute myocarditis
correct_ranking: Primary amebic meningitis with acute myocarditis
